steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Research notes — the goal is to give an overview of the study's findings. The central finding is that although syllable rates varied across the 17 languages (Spanish 7.7/sec, Vietnamese 5.3/sec), the information rate was approximately constant at about 39 bits/sec.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: An overview should convey this main result: despite different syllable rates, all 17 languages conveyed information at roughly the same rate. Choice D captures exactly that contrast.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The 2021 study determined the information rate (IR) of 17 languages in bits of information conveyed per second.': describes what the researchers measured but reports no finding — not an overview of results. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Researchers found that information was conveyed more quickly in Spanish... than in Vietnamese...': conflates syllable rate with information rate and misstates the central finding; the study found IR was approximately equal. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Vietnamese had the sixteenth-fastest syllable rate... Spanish had the lower information rate of the two.': invents a Spanish-vs-Vietnamese IR comparison the notes do not support; the notes say IR was approximately consistent across all 17 languages. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Though some of the languages differed in number of syllables spoken per second, all 17 conveyed information at roughly the same rate.': states the central contrast and the main finding accurately. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
